meta.title: Rhodanese from Thiobacillus A2: Catalysis of Reactions of Thiosulphate with Dihydrolipoate and Dihydrolipoamide

abstractHtml: Summary: Rhodanese (thiosulphate: cyanide sulphurtransferase EC. 2.8.1.1) was purified 25- to 30-fold from thiosulphate-grown Thiobacillus A2. It exhibited a pH optimum between pH 10·2 and 10·4 and apparent KM values of 0·36 mM-Na2S2O3 and 17 mM-KCN. Ultraviolet spectrophotometry and thin-layer chromatography showed that the enzyme catalysed the reaction of S2O32- with dihydrolipoic acid or dihydrolipoamide, producing α-lipoate or lipoamide, with the intermediate production of the persulphides of dihydrolipoate and dihydrolipoamide, which were demonstrated chromatographically. This is the first demonstration of catalysis by a thiobacillus rhodanese of reactions which are likely to be physiologically important in the oxidative dissimilation of thiosulphate by a central energy-conserving pathway.
